icpRubric.notes: Score adoption behavior toward emerging technology, not overall company sophistication. Use definition + assign_when when prompting models or reviewers.

description: Adoption posture toward emerging technology. Score adoption behavior, not company sophistication.

values.label: Early adopter
values.definition: Actively tests, buys, or builds with emerging technology before it is widely proven.
values.assign_when: Evidence shows pilots, experimentation, AI agents, new tooling, custom workflows, innovation teams, or rapid trials.

values.label: Fast follower
values.definition: Adopts proven technology quickly once market validation or competitive pressure exists.
values.assign_when: Evidence shows use of modern tools, references to competitors or benchmarks, or adoption after clear ROI/category momentum.

values.label: Mainstream adopter
values.definition: Uses established, widely accepted technology as part of normal operations.
values.assign_when: Evidence shows a current but not cutting-edge tech stack, pragmatic adoption, stable operations, or standard tooling.

values.label: Late adopter
values.definition: Adopts technology only after it becomes unavoidable, expected, or industry-standard.
values.assign_when: Evidence shows legacy workflows, slow procurement, cautious rollouts, older systems, or low experimentation.

values.label: Technology resistant
values.definition: Avoids, distrusts, or minimizes new technology despite availability.
values.assign_when: Evidence shows explicit skepticism, manual-first culture, compliance/security blockers, or an anti-automation stance.
